title: Pharbitis pubescens
taxonomy: Pharbitis pubescens is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family Convolvulaceae. It is a climbing or sprawling annual plant native to tropical Asia. Pharbitis pubescens is often cultivated for its ornamental value and fragrant flowers. The plant is sometimes referred to as the "fragrant morning glory" due to its sweet-scented blooms. In terms of classification, Pharbitis pubescens is a member of the genus Pharbitis, which is part of the larger family Convolvulaceae.
uses: Pharbitis pubescens, also known as the hairy or velvet mallow, is a plant with reported medicinal uses. The plant's leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues. In some cultures, the plant is used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent to treat wounds and skin conditions.
